entry.title: IEEE Program Helps Girls In India See a Future In STEM
entry.summary: Hands-on projects and role models open doors for STEM careers

entry.title: How global energy production is changing, in 6 charts
entry.summary: Fossil fuels still dominate global electricity, but wind and solar are surging. In 2025, renewables outpaced coal worldwide for the first time, supplying 34% of electricity.

entry.title: We’re Squandering LEDs’ Potential to Save Our Night Skies
entry.summary: Bad decisions have turned efficient technology into a driver of light pollution. There is a better way

entry.title: Ray Kurzweil at AI for Good Global Summit 2026: "We will not be replaced by AI — we will merge with it"
entry.summary: Connect with 55K+ AI enthusiasts

entry.title: This Graduate Student Equips NASA’s Robots With Assembly Skills
entry.summary: Her algorithm enables robots to install antennas on satellites

entry.title: Majorities of Americans say key financial milestones are harder for today’s young adults to reach
entry.summary: Most Americans say it’s harder for young adults to find a job, buy a house, pay for college and save for their future than it was for their parents' generation.

entry.title: Mexicans view President Claudia Sheinbaum favorably, but less so than in 2025
entry.summary: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum remains popular, but her favorability has dropped to 64%. Views of the economy and democracy are also declining.

entry.title: How we measured Americans’ engagement in public life
entry.summary: This piece explains how researchers measured Americans' engagement in public life using multimode surveys, address-based sampling and cluster analysis.

entry.title: How Americans Are Engaged With News, Politics, Religion and Civic Life
entry.summary: Americans’ engagement with others is not one-dimensional. Instead, they sort into four distinct groups that reflect different patterns of participation in society.

entry.title: Younger and older Americans engage in public life in different ways
entry.summary: Older adults are more likely to participate in civic activities like voting, donating or attending religious services, while younger adults engage more in online spaces.

entry.title: Democrats and Republicans engage in public life in similar ways
entry.summary: Each of the two major parties includes people who are deeply involved in public life – across politics, community, religion and news – and those who are less so.

entry.title: Engagement in public life is linked with Americans’ views of politics, news
entry.summary: Americans who participate more in public life are more interested in politics – and more likely to have positive feelings about the state of their local communities.

entry.title: Highly engaged Americans know more about politics
entry.summary: People who are highly engaged in public life are more likely than those who are less engaged to correctly answer several factual questions about politics.

entry.title: Why Indonesia’s Fisheries Future Hinges On Data Integrity and Trust
entry.summary: From vessel tracking to algorithmic enforcement, a surveillance revolution is transforming marine governance

entry.title: Stable Jobs Can Hide the Riskiest Move In Your Tech Career
entry.summary: Strive for better opportunities and cutting-edge skills

entry.title: Notice to Membership
entry.summary: The IEEE Board of Directors sanctions a member

entry.title: Inside ELIZA’s Source Code and Its Multiple Personalities
entry.summary: Uncovering ELIZA’s source code reveals hidden lessons

entry.title: In conversation with Innovate Australia: Peter Kasprzak on supporting the AI for Good Innovation Factory Australia Local Chapter
entry.summary: The AI for Good Innovation Factory reaches its Grand Finale in Geneva each summer, following a year of activity across a large-scale international network. Regional chapters across multiple continents are hosted by organisations with deep knowledge of their local innovation ecosystems, helping to identify promising founders and select the strongest solutions to present before expert juries. With chapters already active in places including India, Catalonia and Malta, the network continues to expand, with Innovate Australia leading the initiative’s local chapter in Australia.

entry.title: Turning Taboo Into Peace with Death: Let's Talk About It | IONS
entry.summary: Marilyn Schlitz presents a wealth of teachings and practical guidance on how to turn a taboo topic into a source of peace.

entry.title: How Darth Vader Taught Me Card Counting and AI Security Got Weird
entry.summary: It only took a little prompting to hijack the biggest AI models

entry.title: Panasonic’s PV-460 Camcorder Stabilized Shaky Videos
entry.summary: Its “anti-jitter” tech is in all modern cameras

entry.title: FIRST Global and Experiential Bring Agentic AI Learning Experience to 190+ Countries, Advancing Robotics Education
entry.summary: FIRST Global Joins the UN ITU AI Skills Coalition; FIRST Global and XRP Kits Offer New Agentic AI module powered by FYI.AI For Student Robotics Teams

entry.title: Vietnam won two second prizes at the global finals of the Robotics for Good 2026 competition
entry.summary: According to information from the STEM Education Promotion Alliance (SEPA) on July 11th, the two Vietnamese teams excellently won second place in the

entry.title: Day 4 at the AI for Good Global Summit: Women's leadership, education and human progress close the 2026 edition
entry.summary: GENEVA, July 10, 2026 - The AI for Good Global Summit concluded following four days of sessions that demonstrated the widespread application of artificial intelligence in a variety of sectors, including health and agriculture, mobility, education, creative industries, and public policy. The summit also raised awareness of quantum technologies, robotics, and the standards and infrastructure required to facilitate their widespread deployment.

entry.title: AI for Good's Youth Zone Wraps Up With a Focus on Responsible Design
entry.summary: GENEVA, 10 July 2026 — Today marked the end of the programme at the AI for Good Global Summit 2026's Youth Zone, which revisited a number of sessions from earlier in the week, and introduced two new sessions on safety and creative problem-solving.

entry.title: The Robotics for Good Youth Challenge 2026 comes to an end as winners are awarded
entry.summary: Connect with 55K+ AI enthusiasts

entry.title: Top 3 things happening on Day 4 of the AI for Good Global Summit 2026
entry.summary: The Robotics for Good Youth Challenge Grand Finale will reach its conclusion on the Youth Stage from 14:10 to 14:45. After four days of competition, the challenge will come to an end, closing a season that opened the Youth Zone's programme on 7 July.

entry.title: Fifteen minutes to convince the committee: meet the ITU Kaleidoscope 2026 winners
entry.summary: The 16th edition of the ITU Kaleidoscope academic conference wrapped up today in Geneva, after three days of peer-reviewed research presentations on AI and frontier technologies. The title of this conference, held under the auspices of the AI for Good Global Summit 2026, aptly reflects the nature of the entire programme: a weave of diverse perspectives emerging from the exchange among researchers from universities, industry and research institutions around the world to discuss how AI, quantum, space and strategic foresight can contribute to advancing sustainable and inclusive development.

entry.title: Consciousness Takes Center Stage: IONS Sponsors The Science of Consciousness 2026 | IONS
entry.summary: IONS is proud to sponsor The Science of Consciousness 2026, the world’s longest-running consciousness conference.

entry.title: Day 3 at the AI for Good Global Summit: Deployment, creative production and youth-led innovation move into focus
entry.summary: Connect with 55K+ AI enthusiasts

entry.title: Tribute to Ervin László – IONS
entry.summary: IONS Board Chair Claudia Welss remembers the extraordinary legacy of Ervin Laszlo.

entry.title: Fourth Annual Noetic Sciences Research Prize to Explore UAP and Consciousness | IONS
entry.summary: The $100,000 Linda G. O’Bryant Prize will recognize bold new research frameworks investigating Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena (UAP).

entry.title: Rewiring Trauma: Why Veterans are Turning to Psychedelics and Holistic Healing for PTSD | IONS
entry.summary: Explore the science behind alternative PTSD relief for veterans, from psychedelic therapies like ibogaine and DMT to holistic methods.

entry.title: What Happens When Three Channelers Describe the Same Extraterrestrials? | IONS
entry.summary: IONS researchers used a structured protocol to compare trance-channeled accounts attributed to extraterrestrial intelligences.

entry.title: Is the Mind Quantum? – IONS
entry.summary: IONS Chief Scientist Dean Radin posits a new way to understand the mind-brain relationship and the implications of that idea.

entry.title: Dean Radin on the Joe Rogan Experience – IONS
entry.summary: Dean Radin sat down with Joe Rogan for a milestone conversation about the science of consciousness.

entry.title: Tech Futures | Montreal AI Ethics Institute
entry.summary: ✍️By Ismael Kherroubi Garcia, Founder & Co-lead of the Responsible Artificial Intelligence Network (RAIN), and Founder & CEO of Kairoi.

entry.title: Celebrating the Legendary Life of Jean Houston – IONS
entry.summary: IONS honors the extraordinary life of Jean Houston.

entry.title: The Twin Flame Journey: A Path to Noetic Wisdom | IONS
entry.summary: Theresa Cheung's latest book explores how the Twin Flame journey may be another language for noetic awakening and wisdom itself.

entry.summary: ✍️ Column by Connor Wright, our Director of Partnerships. Photo Credit: Jackie Hutchinson on Unsplash Overview: Our Director of Partnerships, Connor Wright, was invited to speak at the MISSION Hubs…

entry.title: Recess: Is AI in Law School a Helpful Tool or a Hidden Trap? | Montreal AI Ethics Institute
entry.summary: ✍️By Emma Edney from Encode Canada. Emma is a BCL/JD McCall MacBain Scholar candidate at McGill University. Her interests include ethical issues surrounding personal information in technology and the…

entry.title: Recess: For students at one of Canada’s top universities, how much AI is too much AI when guidelines from professors remain unclear? | Montreal AI Ethics Institute
entry.summary: ✍️By Kennedy O’Neil from Encode Canada. Kennedy is an undergraduate sociology student at McGill University, with a minor in psychology, and a writer for Encode Canada. 📌 Editor’s Note: This piece is…

entry.title: Tech Futures | Montreal AI Ethics Institute
entry.summary: ✍️By Ismael Kherroubi Garcia, Founder & Co-lead of the Responsible Artificial Intelligence Network (RAIN), and Founder & CEO of Kairoi.

entry.title: State of AI Ethics Report Volume 8 (2026): Call for Contributors | Montreal AI Ethics Institute
entry.summary: SAIER Volume 8 (2026) is now open for contributions. We are looking for practitioners, researchers, advocates, policymakers, and community members who are doing the work and who want to help document…

entry.title: How Americans are engaged with news, politics, religion and civic life
entry.summary: Numbers, Facts and Trends Shaping Your World

entry.title: OII | How social media interoperability could restore user choice
entry.summary: Social networking interoperability is a leading policy proposal that gives users a real choice of platform providers. New evidence shows it only works if regulators get the specifics right, argues Philipp Riederle, doctoral researcher, OII.

entry.title: OII | UK adults increasingly seek emotional support and companionship from AI, new report finds
entry.summary: New analysis from researchers at the Oxford Internet Institute reveals that users of artificial intelligence tools are increasingly turning to them for personal support, emotional advice, and companionship.

entry.title: OII | AI-powered social media can subtly manipulate opinion at scale, new study finds
entry.summary: AI tools used to generate, edit or contextualise social media posts can introduce hidden biases that spread through online networks and shape public opinion, according to new research from the OII and the Hasso Plattner Institute.

entry.title: OII | Keeping Europe’s Technological Choices Open
entry.summary: In an age of AI chokepoints, Europe’s choices will depend on the capacities it builds now. The OII's Johanna Ballesteros and TUM Think Tank's DR Nicklas Berild Lundblad explain why.

entry.title: OII | Rethinking EU AI policy: why public subsidies for AI should deliver real wellbeing
entry.summary: A new working paper by OII researchers argues that EU technology policy should hold AI companies to account for actual public wellbeing, not just reduced risk.

entry.title: OII | Expert Comment: Is banning under-16s from social media protection or exclusion?
entry.summary: The OII's Dr Vicki Nash warns that a social media ban risks leaving teenagers 'protected' but disempowered, cut off from news, learning and support at the very moment the UK is preparing to give 16-year-olds the vote.

entry.title: OII | OII researchers head to FAccT 2026
entry.summary: Researchers and DPhil students from the Oxford Internet Institute are set to attend the Association of Computing Machinery (ACM) Conference on Fairness, Accountability and Transparency (FAccT) in Montréal, from 25-28 June 2026.

entry.title: OII | The efficiency paradox in EU data centre policy
entry.summary: New EU reporting rules for data centre energy and water use may look like progress, but loopholes risk undermining genuine environmental accountability.

entry.title: OII | Friendly AI chatbots make more mistakes and tell people what they want to hear, study finds
entry.summary: New Oxford research shows that training chatbots to sound warmer makes them up to 30% less accurate, and 40% more likely to validate users' false beliefs.

entry.title: OII | Why Europe Needs Two Kinds of Digital Sovereignty
entry.summary: Whether Europe shapes the next technological paradigm or simply inherits it depends on one overlooked distinction.

entry.title: China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself
entry.summary: Kimi and other free models from China have again been seen as a wake-up call. But for what?

entry.title: The Download: AI hiring biases, and weather data sabotage
entry.summary: Plus: SpaceX is negotiating to sell the Pentagon AI compute.

entry.title: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
entry.summary: AI doesn’t just learn stereotypes from its training. It can cook up new ones, too.

entry.title: The Download: perimenopause misinformation and China’s latest AI leap
entry.summary: Plus: China's AI gap with the US may have just narrowed.

entry.title: There’s a lot of hype around perimenopause. Don’t buy it.
entry.summary: Discussions of the life stage are often clouded by misinformation.
